spec_match.rationale: Demo exercises the core API surface — every title keyword (after, before, classes, pseudo) and 8 summary keywords appear directly in the page.

interactive.rationale: Pure-CSS interactive surface: 18 in-page anchor links / <details> handles let the user drive the demo without scripted controls — appropriate for CSS-only features.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and concept lede share 12 keywords; the page delivers what the index promises.

edge_cases.rationale: Some defensive coding present (feature-detect or try/catch), but the happy path is dominant — explicit demos of failure modes would round it out.

references.rationale: Page links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5120827674722304 directly in the see-also section.

design_system.rationale: Mostly on-system, but: raw hex colours used (#111, #228b22).

openQuestions.title: Add an explicit edge-case branch
openQuestions.detail: The page covers the happy path. Pick at least one failure mode from the spec (missing permission, empty input, unsupported value, rate-limit, etc.) and wire a button that drives the API into that branch with the resulting error / fallback shown in the page.

openQuestions.title: Fix design-system regressions
openQuestions.detail: Issues found: raw hex colours used (#111, #228b22). Convert raw hex to the existing CSS variables (--bg-ivory, --bg-paper, --bg-stone, --text-black, --text-charcoal, --text-muted, --border-black, --accent-blue, --accent-emerald, --accent-rose) and make sure any button background override also restates color.

summary: No rubric failures remain after adding the ChromeStatus anchor; remaining questions cover edge-case and design-system follow-up.
